_note: Genuine Abū ʿAmr al-Baṣrī (via al-Dūrī) farsh variants where Ḥafṣ and Warsh agree but Abū ʿAmr reads differently. idx = Ḥafṣ word index in the tokenised verse. Arabic forms come from the KFGQPC Dūrī ʿan Abī ʿAmr mushaf; farsh classification per Ibn al-Jazarī, al-Nashr fī al-qirāʾāt al-ʿashr.
cite: Dūrī ʿan Abī ʿAmr (KFGQPC mushaf); farsh per Ibn al-Jazarī, al-Nashr fī al-qirāʾāt al-ʿashr.

entries.shared: no intercession will be accepted from it (yuqbalu — masculine passive)
entries.abuamr: no intercession will be accepted from it (tuqbalu — feminine passive, agreeing with shafāʿa)
of what they do (yaʿmalūna — 3rd person)
then a pledge (ruhun — an alternative plural of rahn)
we teach him (nuʿallimuh — 1st person plural)

entries.shared: of what you do (taʿmalūna — 2nd person plural)
entries.abuamr: of what they do (yaʿmalūna — 3rd person plural)

entries.shared: you will surely make it clear (la-tubayyinunnahu — 2nd person)
entries.abuamr: he will surely make it clear (la-yubayyinunnahu — 3rd person)
they conceal it (yaktumūnahū — 3rd person)
he supposes about them (yahsibunnahum — 3rd person with kasra)
he gives him (yuʾtīhi — 3rd person masculine)
of what they understand (yaʿqilūn — 3rd person)

entries.shared: He relates the truth (yaquṣṣu — from qaṣṣa, to narrate)
entries.abuamr: He decides by the truth (yaqḍi — from qaḍā, to judge/decree)
they make it (yajʿalūnahū — 3rd person)
they reveal it (yubdūnahā — 3rd person)
and they conceal (wa-yakhfūn — 3rd person)

entries.shared: your errors (khaṭīʾātikum — feminine plural noun form)
entries.abuamr: your errors (khaṭāyākum — alternative plural of the error root)
of what they understand (yaʿqilūn — 3rd person)
they say (yaqūlū — 3rd person plural)
they say (yaqūlū — 3rd person plural)
will be (takūn — 2nd person masculine singular)
of what they do (yaʿmalūna — 3rd person)

entries.shared: he may pasture and enjoy himself (yartaʿ — jussive, of Joseph)
entries.abuamr: we may pasture and enjoy ourselves (nartaʿ — the brothers, 1st-person plural)
and we may play (nalʿab)
of what they understand (yaʿqilūn — 3rd person)
incline/turn (tatafayyaʾu — feminine, agreeing with ẓilāl, ‘shadows’)
they take (yattakhidhū — 3rd person plural)

entries.shared: that He should cause the shore to swallow you (yakhsifa — He)
entries.abuamr: that We should cause the shore to swallow you (nakhsifa — We)
or that We send against you (nursila — We)

entries.shared: He restores you (yuʿīdukum — 3rd person masculine, divine voice)
entries.abuamr: We restore you (nuʿīdukum — 1st person plural, divine 'We')

entries.shared: or He sends (yursil — 3rd person masculine, divine voice)
entries.abuamr: or We send (nursil — 1st person plural, divine 'We')

entries.shared: or He drowns you (yughriqakum — 3rd person masculine, divine voice)
entries.abuamr: or We drown you (nughriqakum — 1st person plural, divine 'We')

entries.shared: We set them in motion (nusayyiru — 1st person plural)
entries.abuamr: it is set in motion (tusayyaru — passive)

entries.shared: almost burst apart (yatafaṭṭarna — form V, from faṭara)
entries.abuamr: burst apart (yanfaṭirna — form VII, from faṭara)
these two (hādhayni — accusative dual, the case grammar expects after inna)
We blow the Trumpet (nanfukhu — 1st-person plural)
Allah repels / defends (yadfaʿu — form I)
you destroyed it (ahlaktuhā — 2nd person singular masculine)

entries.shared: striving to frustrate Our signs (muʿājizīn — form III)
entries.abuamr: seeking to thwart Our signs (muʿajjizīn — form II; sense is close)
blazes up (tawaqqada — form V)
they remember (yadhakkarūn — 3rd person plural)

entries.shared: of what you do (tafʿalūn — 2nd person plural)
entries.abuamr: of what they do (yafʿalūn — 3rd person plural)

entries.shared: of what you do (taʿmalūn — 2nd person plural)
entries.abuamr: of what they do (yaʿmalūn — 3rd person plural)
of what they understand (yaʿqilūn — 3rd person)
and We say (wa-naqūl — 1st person plural)

entries.shared: you are returned (turjaʿūn — 2nd person plural passive)
entries.abuamr: they are returned (yurjaʿūn — 3rd person plural passive)

entries.shared: of what you do (taʿmalūn — 2nd person plural)
entries.abuamr: of what they do (yaʿmalūn — 3rd person plural)

entries.shared: of what you do (taʿmalūn — 2nd person plural)
entries.abuamr: of what they do (yaʿmalūn — 3rd person plural)
it is lawful (taḥillu — feminine, agreeing with an-nisāʾ, ‘women’)

entries.shared: you are promised (tūʿadūn — 2nd person plural passive)
entries.abuamr: they are promised (yūʿadūn — 3rd person plural passive)
it will avail (tanfaʿ — 3rd person feminine, grammatical gender agreement)

entries.shared: almost burst apart (yatafaṭṭarna — form V, from faṭara)
entries.abuamr: burst apart (yanfaṭirna — form VII, from faṭara)

entries.shared: you desire it (tashtahīhi — 2nd person with attached object pronoun)
entries.abuamr: you desire (tashtahī — 2nd person without object pronoun, general desiring)
they believe (yuʾminū — 3rd person plural)

entries.shared: and you honor him (wa-tuʿazzirūh — 2nd person plural)
entries.abuamr: and they honor him (wa-yuʿazzirūh — 3rd person plural)

entries.shared: and you respect him (wa-tuwaqqirūh — 2nd person plural)
entries.abuamr: and they respect him (wa-yuwaqqirūh — 3rd person plural)

entries.shared: and you glorify him (wa-tusabbihūh — 2nd person plural)
entries.abuamr: and they glorify him (wa-yusabbihūh — 3rd person plural)

entries.shared: of what you do (taʿmalūn — 2nd person plural)
entries.abuamr: of what they do (yaʿmalūn — 3rd person plural)

entries.shared: and their descendants followed them (wa-ittabaʿathum — form VIII, they followed)
entries.abuamr: and We caused their descendants to follow them (wa-atbaʿnāhum — form IV causative, 1st person plural active)

entries.shared: their errors (khaṭīʾātihim — feminine plural noun form)
entries.abuamr: their errors (khaṭāyāhum — alternative plural of the error root)
they love (yuhibbūn — 3rd person plural)
and they neglect (wa-yadhārūn — 3rd person plural)
they will (yashāʾūn — 3rd person plural)
they prefer (yuʾthirūn — 3rd person plural)
is heard (yusmaʿu — passive)
they honor (yukrimūn — 3rd person plural)
and they eat (wa-yaʾkulūn — 3rd person plural)
and they love (wa-yuhibbūn — 3rd person plural)
